title: striga bilabiata
intro: Striga bilabiata, also known as the African witchweed, is a flowering plant native to tropical Africa, characterized by its yellow or orange flowers and small, bilabiate leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal and anti-ulcer agent. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves or flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
